changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for specificity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to monitoring and controlling usage. However, some naming improvements were necessary for clarity and consistency with existing patterns. The audit logging function should specify the API server as the resource, and the network policy function should clearly indicate it is about traffic monitoring.
naming_improvements: Use 'apiserver' instead of 'audit' for audit logging to specify the resource
Clarify 'monitoring' in network policies to specify 'traffic' monitoring
title: Usage Restrictions - Authorize, monitor, and control the...
step1_reasoning: The control SC-43-b focuses on authorizing, monitoring, and controlling usage. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring audit logging is enabled to monitor API activities, using admission controllers to enforce security policies, applying NetworkPolicies to control and monitor network traffic, and configuring RBAC to monitor and control access activities.
